message:
feat(tokio-parity): close 6 I/O gaps, add reactor contract T2.6, expand web/gRPC and fs/signal coverage

Major progress on the tokio replacement parity tracking:

I/O parity (T2.2/T2.3):
- Close gaps IO-G1 (read), IO-G3 (integer read), IO-G4 (write),
  IO-G6 (integer write) from prior AsyncReadExt/WriteExt work
- Close ReaderStream + StreamReader gaps; narrow IO-G12 to SinkWriter only
- Update gap summary: 14 total, 6 closed, 8 remaining

Reactor backend parity (T2.6):
- Add cross-backend contract matrix R09.1-R09.6 covering epoll, kqueue,
  IOCP, and io_uring with source/test evidence anchors
- Add drift-detection rules for reactor readiness consistency

Web/gRPC parity (T5):
- Add warp framework surface coverage (filter combinators, typed paths,
  reject/recover) alongside existing axum/actix/tonic
- Bump minimum gap count from 30 to 40
- Add T5.2 router closure scope contracts (T52-ROUTE-01 through T52-ROUTE-06)
- Add ownership boundaries and revision history tracking

Filesystem/process/signal (T3):
- Add machine-readable JSON companion for parity matrix
- Add drift-detection rules T3-DRIFT-01 through T3-DRIFT-05
- Add T3.5 executable cross-platform signal contract pack (SGC-01-SGC-04)
- Expand contract tests to 10 new scenarios with JSON/markdown sync checks

Also updates capability evidence map, risk register, ecosystem inventory,
functional parity contract (T1.2 domain DoD synthesis), evidence checklist,
and nonfunctional closure criteria with cross-references.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+| R09.5 | Wake path supports poll interruption without duplicate user-level wake dispatch | `Poller::notify()` | `Poller::notify()` | `Poller::notify()` | eventfd wake channel (`WAKE_USER_DATA`) |

+| `T52-ROUTE-02` | Router composition (`merge`) is deterministic | Merged routers dispatch in stable precedence order | Duplicate route/method collision emits explicit conflict diagnostics | Cancel before dispatch returns cancelled outcome without invoking handler | Deterministic tie-break key is route registration index |
+| `T52-ROUTE-03` | Fallback resolution semantics | Unmatched request resolves to configured fallback chain | Missing fallback returns explicit 404 contract response | Cancel during fallback body generation drains response task | Fallback decision path recorded with route fingerprint |
+| `T52-ROUTE-04` | Method dispatch correctness | Supported method maps to exact route handler | Unsupported method yields 405 with allow-list metadata | Cancel between method check and handler poll returns cancelled outcome | Method map and allow-list are stable across runs |
+| `T52-ROUTE-05` | Wildcard + parameter extraction precedence | Most-specific route wins over wildcard captures | Ambiguous patterns surface deterministic conflict status | Cancel during extraction does not leak partial param state | Extracted param set hash is stable for same path input |
